clusters.title: EU leaders vent anger after Hungary's Orban keeps blocking Ukraine loan

items.title: EU leaders vent anger after Hungary's Orban keeps blocking Ukraine loan
items.standfirst: European Union leaders on Thursday lashed out at Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, accusing him of hijacking critical aid for Ukraine and undermining EU decision-making in an effort to win an election at home. France 24's correspondent Dave Keating has the latest from Brussels.
items.content: European Union leaders on Thursday lashed out at Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, accusing him of hijacking critical aid for Ukraine and undermining EU decision-making in an effort to win an election at home. France 24's correspondent Dave Keating has the latest from Brussels.

items.title: Outrage among EU leaders as Hungary's Orban U-turns on Ukraine loan
items.standfirst: Outraged EU leaders rounded on Viktor Orban after the Hungarian nationalist refused to lift his veto on funds for Ukraine's war effort at a Thursday summit, accusing him of a "gross act of disloyalty" tantamount to "blackmail." It is not the first time Orban has blocked aid to Ukraine, but the anger has been magnified by the fact the Hungarian leader had previously agreed to the deal, says France 24's Kethevane Gorjestani.
items.content: Outraged EU leaders rounded on Viktor Orban after the Hungarian nationalist refused to lift his veto on funds for Ukraine's war effort at a Thursday summit, accusing him of a "gross act of disloyalty" tantamount to "blackmail." It is not the first time Orban has blocked aid to Ukraine, but the anger has been magnified by the fact the Hungarian leader had previously agreed to the deal, says France 24's Kethevane Gorjestani.

items.title: Viktor Orbán refuses to agree to €90bn loan for Ukraine as EU leaders accuse him of betrayal
items.standfirst: German chancellor Friedrich Merz described Orbán’s U-turn on the loan Hungary had agreed to in December as ‘a gross act of disloyalty’EU leaders fumed after Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, refused to drop his opposition to a vital €90bn (£78bn) loan for Ukraine, accusing him of betrayal and acting in bad faith.German chancellor Friedrich Merz described Orbán’s U-turn on the loan Hungary had agreed to in December as “a gross act of disloyalty” adding: “I am firmly convinced that it will leave deep marks.” Continue reading...
items.content: German chancellor Friedrich Merz described Orbán’s U-turn on the loan Hungary had agreed to in December as ‘a gross act of disloyalty’EU leaders fumed after Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, refused to drop his opposition to a vital €90bn (£78bn) loan for Ukraine, accusing him of betrayal and acting in bad faith.German chancellor Friedrich Merz described Orbán’s U-turn on the loan Hungary had agreed to in December as “a gross act of disloyalty” adding: “I am firmly convinced that it will leave deep marks.” Continue reading...

ai_summary: EU leaders lash out at Hungarian Prime Minister Orban
Orban blocks Ukraine loan despite previous agreement
EU leaders accuse Orban of disloyalty and blackmail
Germany's chancellor describes Orban's actions as disloyal
EU leaders frustrated with Orban's opposition to loan

items.title: Household energy bills in Great Britain ‘could rise to almost £2,000 a year’ amid Iran war shock
items.standfirst: Consultancy forecasts typical £1,972 annual dual fuel bill as conflict pushes UK’s gas market past three-year highsBusiness live – latest updatesHousehold energy bills in Great Britain could soar by more than £330 a year to almost £2,000 from this summer after the war in Iran pushed the UK’s gas market past three-year highs.A typical combined household gas and electricity bill is now forecast to reach £1,972 a year from July under the UK government’s quarterly price cap, according to analysis by Cornwall Insight, an energy consultancy. items.title: Venezuela interim president replaces top military commanders in sweeping reforms
items.standfirst: Venezuela’s interim President Delcy Rodriguez said on Thursday she had replaced all senior military commanders, expanding sweeping reforms after the US ousted Nicolas Maduro. The move follows the dismissal of a long-serving defence minister and the appointment of a former intelligence chief, as Caracas reshapes its armed forces.
items.content: Venezuela’s interim President Delcy Rodriguez said on Thursday she had replaced all senior military commanders, expanding sweeping reforms after the US ousted Nicolas Maduro. items.title: BTS release new album Arirang ahead of comeback concert
items.standfirst: Boyband drops album that speaks to its Korean roots ahead of Seoul comeback concert, with more than a quarter of a million fans expected to attendK-pop stars BTS released a new album on Friday billed as reflecting the maturing boy band’s Korean roots and identity, as buzz built ahead of their open-air comeback concert in the heart of Seoul.The Saturday night gig, which is expected to draw around 260,000 people, will be BTS’s first after a hiatus of almost four years while all seven members served compulsory military service. It comes ahead of an 82-date world tour. Continue reading...
items.content: Boyband drops album that speaks to its Korean roots ahead of Seoul comeback concert, with more than a quarter of a million fans expected to attendK-pop stars BTS released a new album on Friday billed as reflecting the maturing boy band’s Korean roots and identity, as buzz built ahead of their open-air comeback concert in the heart of Seoul.The Saturday night gig, which is expected to draw around 260,000 people, will be BTS’s first after a hiatus of almost four years while all seven members served compulsory military service. It comes ahead of an 82-date world tour. Continue reading...

items.title: K-pop megastars BTS drop new album ahead of comeback mega-concert
items.standfirst: After an almost four-year hiatus, K-pop icons BTS dropped a new album Friday ahead of an open-air concert in Seoul expected to draw more than a quarter of a million fans from around the world.
items.content: After an almost four-year hiatus, K-pop icons BTS dropped a new album Friday ahead of an open-air concert in Seoul expected to draw more than a quarter of a million fans from around the world.

ai_summary: BTS releases new album reflecting Korean roots
Album drops ahead of Seoul comeback concert
Over 260,000 fans expected to attend the concert
Concert marks BTS's first after four-year hiatus
Band to embark on 82-date world tour

clusters.title: Middle East war live: US-Israeli air strikes kill Iran’s Revolutionary Guard spokesperson
clusters.neutral_headline: US Israeli Air Strikes Kill Iran Revolutionary Guard Spokesperson

items.title: Middle East war live: US-Israeli air strikes kill Iran’s Revolutionary Guard spokesperson
items.standfirst: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps spokesperson Ali Mohammad Naini was killed in dawn US-Israeli air strikes on Friday, state media reported. The strikes came as an Iranian drone attack hit Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi oil refinery, causing several fires but no casualties. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps spokesperson Ali Mohammad Naini was killed in dawn US-Israeli air strikes on Friday, state media reported. The strikes came as an Iranian drone attack hit Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi oil refinery, causing several fires but no casualties. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Iran war live: IRGC says spokesman killed in US-Israeli missile attack
items.standfirst: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says its spokesperson has been killed in a US-Israeli missile attack.
items.content: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says its spokesperson has been killed in a US-Israeli missile attack.

ai_summary: Iranian drone attack hits Kuwait oil refinery
US-Israeli air strikes kill IRGC spokesperson
Strikes cause several fires but no casualties
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps confirms death
Attack comes amid escalating Middle East conflict

clusters.title: Iran hangs 3, including teen wrestler, in first executions over Jan. protests
clusters.neutral_headline: Iran Executes Three Men Over January Protests

items.title: Iran hangs 3, including teen wrestler, in first executions over Jan. protests
items.standfirst: Two sources confirmed to CBS News that Saleh Mohammadi, a young member of Iran's national wrestling team, was among the three men executed in Iran.
items.content: Two sources confirmed to CBS News that Saleh Mohammadi, a young member of Iran's national wrestling team, was among the three men executed in Iran.

items.title: Teenager among first to be executed over Iran's anti-government protests
items.standfirst: Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say.
items.content: Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say.

ai_summary: Iran hangs three men including teen wrestler
Executions are first over January anti-government protests
Saleh Mohammadi was a member of Iran's wrestling team
Two other men were also found guilty of killing police
State media reports on the executions

clusters.title: China has been preparing for a global energy crisis for years. It is paying off now
clusters.neutral_headline: China Has Been Preparing For Global Energy Crisis

items.title: China has been preparing for a global energy crisis for years. It is paying off now
items.standfirst: As other Asian economies race to conserve energy, China has huge reserves of oil and gas as well as alternative energy sources like wind and solarMiddle East crisis – live updatesXi Jinping has been preparing for a crisis like this for years. China must secure its energy supply “in its own hands”, its president was reported to have said during a visit to one of its vast oilfields in 2021.The US-Israel war on Iran plunged the Middle East into a deep conflict, with the strait of Hormuz – one of the most important waterways in global trade – all but closed and key energy facilities across the region under attack. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran says it will show ‘zero restraint’ if energy infrastructure is targeted again
items.standfirst: Foreign minister issues warning after Israeli attack on South Pars gasfield and as Qatar reels from retaliatory strike‘Doomsday scenario’: a visual guide to the oil and gas site attacks in the Middle EastMiddle East crisis – live updatesIran said on Thursday it would show “zero restraint” if its energy infrastructure was targeted again as Qatar revealed that almost a fifth of its liquefied natural gas export capacity had been knocked out in an Iranian strike that is likely to have a years-long impact.The warning, delivered by the Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, followed Israel’s attack on Iran’s massive South Pars gasfield – which it shares with Qatar – which triggered Iranian retaliatory strikes on Qatar’s Ras Laffan gas complex and other Gulf neighbours, sending stock markets tumbling globally and triggering sharp increases in gas prices. Continue reading...
items.content: Foreign minister issues warning after Israeli attack on South Pars gasfield and as Qatar reels from retaliatory strike‘Doomsday scenario’: a visual guide to the oil and gas site attacks in the Middle EastMiddle East crisis – live updatesIran said on Thursday it would show “zero restraint” if its energy infrastructure was targeted again as Qatar revealed that almost a fifth of its liquefied natural gas export capacity had been knocked out in an Iranian strike that is likely to have a years-long impact.The warning, delivered by the Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, followed Israel’s attack on Iran’s massive South Pars gasfield – which it shares with Qatar – which triggered Iranian retaliatory strikes on Qatar’s Ras Laffan gas complex and other Gulf neighbours, sending stock markets tumbling globally and triggering sharp increases in gas prices. Continue reading...

items.title: ‘Doomsday scenario’: a visual guide to the oil and gas site attacks in the Middle East
items.standfirst: Attacks on facilities by both sides in the conflict this week threaten grave consequences for the global economyMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe escalating attacks on key oil and gas projects in the Middle East are expected to fuel a new phase of the ongoing conflict, with profound consequences for the world’s energy supplies and the global economy.The Iran regime has vowed to target a string of key energy infrastructure across the region after warning that an Israeli strike on a production facility for its largest gasfield at South Pars on Wednesday had ignited a “full-scale economic war”. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Oil and gas prices jump after Iran and Israel attack gasfields
items.standfirst: State-run QatarEnergy says Iranian attack on its facilities has wiped out 17% of its LNG capacity for up to five yearsMiddle East crisis – live updatesBusiness live – latest updatesGas prices jumped to four-year highs and oil prices rose again after an escalation of attacks by Israel and Iran on gasfields heightened fears of prolonged disruption to international energy supplies.QatarEnergy told Reuters on Thursday that Iran had damaged facilities that produced 17% of the state-owned company’s liquefied natural gas (LNG) export capacity and that it would take three to five years to repair them. Continue reading...
items.content: State-run QatarEnergy says Iranian attack on its facilities has wiped out 17% of its LNG capacity for up to five yearsMiddle East crisis – live updatesBusiness live – latest updatesGas prices jumped to four-year highs and oil prices rose again after an escalation of attacks by Israel and Iran on gasfields heightened fears of prolonged disruption to international energy supplies.QatarEnergy told Reuters on Thursday that Iran had damaged facilities that produced 17% of the state-owned company’s liquefied natural gas (LNG) export capacity and that it would take three to five years to repair them. Continue reading...

Alternative energy sources include wind and solar
US-Israel war on Iran affects global trade
Strait of Hormuz is a key waterway in global trade
China's energy supply is a priority for the government
